message:
feat(query): implement joined-table inheritance DML routing

Major expansion of the query builder to support INSERT, UPDATE, and
DELETE for joined-table inheritance child models (542+/51-):

- classify_joined_column(): routes each column to parent or child
  table, supporting qualified (table.col) and unqualified names
  with ambiguity detection for overlapping column names
- build_insert_sql_for_table(): generates per-table INSERT with
  dialect-aware placeholder numbering
- InsertBuilder: splits row values across parent and child tables,
  executes parent INSERT first (within transaction) then child INSERT,
  handles RETURNING by projecting table__col aliases from both tables
- InsertManyBuilder: batch-inserts rows split across parent/child
  tables within a single transaction
- UpdateBuilder: routes SET columns to parent/child, generates
  separate UPDATE statements with shared WHERE clause
- DeleteBuilder: deletes child rows first then parent rows (FK order),
  supports RETURNING via pre-delete SELECT with joined projection

All DML operations use transactions to maintain referential integrity.
Includes comprehensive unit tests for column classification and
SQL generation across SQLite/Postgres/MySQL dialects.

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>
